title: Architect explaining sustainable design to city planners
title_zh: 建筑师向城市规划者阐述可持续设计

tree.setup: You are a lead architect presenting a sustainable design proposal for a new civic center to a municipal planning commission in a major Chinese city. Your design integrates green building technology with traditional Chinese architectural principles. The commission members are skeptical about costs and practicality, and one member has just questioned whether sustainability features are merely decorative rather than functionally necessary.
tree.setup_zh: 你是一位首席建筑师，正在向某大城市市政规划委员会展示一个新市民中心的可持续设计方案。你的设计将绿色建筑技术与中国传统建筑原则相融合。委员们对成本和实用性持怀疑态度，一位委员刚刚质疑可持续性要素是否只是装饰性的而非功能性必需的。

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 李建筑师，坦白讲，我看了你们的方案之后，有一个根本性的疑问。你们提的那些绿色技术——屋顶花园、太阳能幕墙、雨水回收系统——听起来确实很先进。但作为纳税人资金的管理者，我必须问：这些东西到底是在解决真正的功能需求，还是只是在给建筑贴一个'环保标签'来迎合当前的政策风向？我需要看到硬数据——投入产出比、能耗节约的具体数字、以及与传统方案的全生命周期成本对比。
turns.text_pinyin: lǐ jiànzhùshī, tǎnbái jiǎng, wǒ kàn le nǐmen de fāng'àn zhīhòu, yǒu yī gè gēnběn xìng de yíwèn. nǐmen tí de nàxiē lǜsè jìshù — wūdǐng huāyuán, tàiyángnéng mùqiáng, yǔshuǐ huíshōu xìtǒng — tīng qǐlái quèshí hěn xiānjìn. dàn zuòwéi nàshuì rén zījīn de guǎnlǐ zhě, wǒ bìxū wèn: zhèxiē dōngxī dàodǐ shì zài jiějué zhēnzhèng de gōngnéng xūqiú, háishì zhǐ shì zài gěi jiànzhù tiē yī gè 'huánbǎo biāoqiān' lái yínghé dāngqián de zhèngcè fēngxiàng? wǒ xūyào kàndào yìng shùjù — tóurù chǎnchū bǐ, néng hào jiéyuē de jùtǐ shùzì, yǐjí yǔ chuántǒng fāng'àn de quán shēngmìng zhōuqī chéngběn duìbǐ.
turns.text_en: Architect Li, frankly speaking, after reviewing your proposal, I have a fundamental question. The green technologies you've proposed — rooftop gardens, solar curtain walls, rainwater recycling systems — certainly sound advanced. But as managers of taxpayer funds, I must ask: are these things actually solving real functional needs, or just slapping an 'eco-label' on the building to align with current policy trends? I need to see hard data — input-output ratios, specific figures on energy savings, and a full life-cycle cost comparison with conventional approaches.

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Address the skeptical planner's demand for hard data on sustainability ROI.

options.text_zh: 王委员，您提出的质疑非常务实，我完全认同——可持续设计如果不能通过经济账来论证，就停留在口号层面。请允许我用数据来回应。首先，关于全生命周期成本：根据我们与同济大学建筑节能研究中心的联合测算，本方案的初始建设成本比传统方案高出约百分之十五，但在五十年的运营周期内，仅空调能耗一项就可节省约四千三百万元——这得益于太阳能幕墙的被动式调温功能和屋顶花园的隔热效应，综合节能率达到百分之三十七。加上雨水回收系统每年可替代约六万吨市政供水，按当前水价计算，全生命周期的净节省额超过一亿两千万元——相当于初期增量投入的四点六倍。其次，这些技术绝非装饰性的。太阳能幕墙不是'贴上去的太阳能板'，而是建筑围护结构的有机组成部分——它同时承担发电、遮阳和通风三重功能。屋顶花园也不仅仅是景观，它是城市热岛效应的缓解机制，经实测可将屋顶表面温度降低十五到二十度。最后，关于政策风向——我想提醒各位，住建部最新发布的《绿色建筑评价标准》已经将碳排放强度纳入了公共建筑的强制性指标。这意味着可持续设计不是'锦上添花'，而是未来十年所有公共建筑的底线要求。
options.text_pinyin: wáng wěiyuán, nín tíchū de zhìyí fēicháng wùshí, wǒ wánquán rèntóng — kě chíxù shèjì rúguǒ bù néng tōngguò jīngjì zhàng lái lùnzhèng, jiù tíngliú zài kǒuhào céngmiàn. qǐng yǔnxǔ wǒ yòng shùjù lái huíyìng. shǒuxiān, guānyú quán shēngmìng zhōuqī chéngběn: gēnjù wǒmen yǔ tóngjì dàxué jiànzhù jiénéng yánjiū zhōngxīn de liánhé cèsuàn, běn fāng'àn de chūshǐ jiànshè chéngběn bǐ chuántǒng fāng'àn gāo chū yuē bǎifēnzhī shíwǔ, dàn zài wǔshí nián de yùnyíng zhōuqī nèi, jǐn kōngtiáo néng hào yī xiàng jiù kě jiéshěng yuē sì qiān sān bǎi wàn yuán — zhè déyì yú tàiyángnéng mùqiáng de bèidòng shì tiáowēn gōngnéng hé wūdǐng huāyuán de gérè xiàoyìng, zōnghé jiénéng lǜ dádào bǎifēnzhī sānshí qī. jiāshàng yǔshuǐ huíshōu xìtǒng měi nián kě tìdài yuē liù wàn dūn shìzhèng gōngshuǐ, àn dāngqián shuǐjià jìsuàn, quán shēngmìng zhōuqī de jìng jiéshěng é chāoguò yī yì liǎng qiān wàn yuán — xiāngdāng yú chūqī zēngliàng tóurù de sì diǎn liù bèi. qícì, zhèxiē jìshù juéfēi zhuāngshì xìng de. tàiyángnéng mùqiáng bú shì 'tiē shàngqù de tàiyángnéng bǎn', ér shì jiànzhù wéihù jiégòu de yǒujī zǔchéng bùfèn — tā tóngshí chéngdān fādiàn, zhēyáng hé tōngfēng sān chóng gōngnéng. wūdǐng huāyuán yě bùjǐnjǐn shì jǐngguān, tā shì chéngshì rèdǎo xiàoyìng de huǎnjiě jīzhì, jīng shícè kě jiāng wūdǐng biǎomiàn wēndù jiàngdī shíwǔ dào èrshí dù. zuìhòu, guānyú zhèngcè fēngxiàng — wǒ xiǎng tíxǐng gèwèi, zhùjiàn bù zuìxīn fābù de 'lǜsè jiànzhù píngjià biāozhǔn' yǐjīng jiāng tàn páifàng qiángdù nàrù le gōnggòng jiànzhù de qiángzhì xìng zhǐbiāo. zhè yìwèi zhe kě chíxù shèjì bú shì 'jǐnshàng tiānhuā', ér shì wèilái shí nián suǒyǒu gōnggòng jiànzhù de dǐxiàn yāoqiú.
options.text_en: Commissioner Wang, your skepticism is very pragmatic, and I fully agree — if sustainable design can't be justified through economic analysis, it remains at the slogan level. Allow me to respond with data. First, regarding full life-cycle costs: based on our joint assessment with Tongji University's Building Energy Conservation Research Center, this proposal's initial construction cost is approximately 15% higher than conventional approaches, but over a 50-year operational cycle, air conditioning energy savings alone reach approximately 43 million yuan — thanks to the solar curtain wall's passive temperature regulation and rooftop garden insulation effects, achieving a comprehensive energy-saving rate of 37%. Adding the rainwater recycling system's annual replacement of approximately 60,000 tons of municipal water supply, calculated at current water rates, the net savings over the full life cycle exceed 120 million yuan — equivalent to 4.6 times the initial incremental investment. Second, these technologies are absolutely not decorative. The solar curtain wall isn't 'solar panels stuck on' — it's an organic component of the building envelope, simultaneously serving triple functions of power generation, sun shading, and ventilation. Rooftop gardens aren't merely landscaping either — they're a mitigation mechanism for the urban heat island effect, with measured reductions of 15 to 20 degrees in rooftop surface temperature. Finally, regarding policy trends — I'd like to remind everyone that the Ministry of Housing and Urban-Rural Development's latest Green Building Evaluation Standard has incorporated carbon emission intensity as a mandatory indicator for public buildings. This means sustainable design isn't 'icing on the cake' but the baseline requirement for all public buildings over the next decade.
options.feedback: Data-driven and persuasive — uses 全生命周期成本, 投入产出比, 被动式调温, 围护结构, 综合节能率, 碳排放强度 with professional command. The three-part structure (economic ROI, functional integration, regulatory inevitability) addresses every dimension of the skeptic's concern. Specific numbers (15% premium, 37% energy savings, 4.6x return) demonstrate rigorous analysis. Ending with regulatory requirements reframes sustainability from optional to mandatory — a strategic rhetorical move.

options.text_zh: 可持续设计确实成本会高一些，但长期来看是值得的。现在国家也在推广绿色建筑，我们应该跟上潮流。屋顶花园和太阳能都能节省能源。
options.text_pinyin: kě chíxù shèjì quèshí chéngběn huì gāo yīxiē, dàn chángqī lái kàn shì zhídé de. xiànzài guójiā yě zài tuīguǎng lǜsè jiànzhù, wǒmen yīnggāi gēn shàng cháoliú. wūdǐng huāyuán hé tàiyángnéng dōu néng jiéshěng néngyuán.
options.text_en: Sustainable design does cost a bit more, but it's worth it in the long run. The country is also promoting green buildings now, we should keep up with the trend. Rooftop gardens and solar energy can both save energy.
options.feedback: Generalities won't satisfy a commissioner demanding 硬数据. Saying 'it costs more but is worth it' without specific 全生命周期成本 figures, and 'the country is promoting it' without citing specific standards (绿色建筑评价标准), reads as evasion. An architect presenting to planners must command 投入产出比, 综合节能率, and regulatory timelines.

options.text_zh: 这些绿色技术是全球趋势，不用就会落后。其他国家的新建筑都在用这些技术，我们不能落后于国际水平。
options.text_pinyin: zhèxiē lǜsè jìshù shì quánqiú qūshì, bú yòng jiù huì luòhòu. qítā guójiā de xīn jiànzhù dōu zài yòng zhèxiē jìshù, wǒmen bù néng luòhòu yú guójì shuǐpíng.
options.text_en: These green technologies are a global trend, not using them means falling behind. Other countries' new buildings are all using these technologies, we can't fall behind international standards.
options.feedback: Appeals to vague international comparisons without data. The commissioner explicitly asked for 投入产出比 and 全生命周期成本对比 — answering with 'global trend' and 'can't fall behind' dodges the core question. At a planning commission, demonstrate technical mastery with specific 节能率, 碳排放, and cost recovery timelines, not slogans about international trends.

turns.speaker: npc
turns.text_zh: 好，数据方面你说服了我。但我还有另一个顾虑——你提到了中国传统建筑原则的融合。这个概念在PPT上看起来很美，但在实际操作中，传统元素和现代绿色技术之间会不会存在矛盾？比如说，传统的中式屋顶形制和太阳能板的安装需求之间怎么协调？我不希望最后搞出一个'不中不西'的东西。
turns.text_pinyin: hǎo, shùjù fāngmiàn nǐ shuōfú le wǒ. dàn wǒ hái yǒu lìng yī gè gùlǜ — nǐ tídào le zhōngguó chuántǒng jiànzhù yuánzé de rónghé. zhège gàiniàn zài PPT shàng kàn qǐlái hěn měi, dàn zài shíjì cāozuò zhōng, chuántǒng yuánsù hé xiàndài lǜsè jìshù zhījiān huì bú huì cúnzài máodùn? bǐrú shuō, chuántǒng de zhōngshì wūdǐng xíngzhì hé tàiyángnéng bǎn de ānzhuāng xūqiú zhījiān zěnme xiétiáo? wǒ bù xīwàng zuìhòu gǎo chū yī gè 'bù zhōng bù xī' de dōngxī.
turns.text_en: OK, you've convinced me on the data front. But I have another concern — you mentioned integration of traditional Chinese architectural principles. This concept looks beautiful on PowerPoint, but in practice, won't there be contradictions between traditional elements and modern green technology? For example, how do you reconcile traditional Chinese roof forms with solar panel installation requirements? I don't want to end up with something that's 'neither Chinese nor Western.'

turns.speaker: player
turns.prompt_en: Explain how traditional Chinese architecture and green technology can be integrated without aesthetic compromise.

options.text_zh: 这个问题正好触及了我们设计理念的核心。我要坦诚地说——如果只是在现代建筑上贴几片琉璃瓦，那确实是'不中不西'。但我们的方法论完全不同。我们的出发点不是传统建筑的表面形式，而是其背后的空间智慧。举个例子：中国传统建筑中的'天井'——那种垂直的通风采光空间——其实就是最早的被动式建筑设计。我们在市民中心的中庭设计中复活了这个原理，创造了一个贯穿六层楼的'现代天井'，利用热压通风原理实现自然换气，将机械通风能耗降低百分之四十。再比如，您提到的屋顶问题——我们没有在传统坡屋顶上硬装太阳能板。相反，我们借鉴了宋代建筑中'举折'的曲线美学，将薄膜太阳能电池直接整合到屋面材料中，使其成为屋顶曲面的一部分。从外观上看，它呈现的是优美的中式屋顶轮廓；从功能上看，弧面设计反而增加了全天候的日照接收面积，比平面安装效率提高了百分之十二。这不是中西方的拼贴，而是古今之间的对话——用传统智慧解决当代问题。
options.text_pinyin: zhège wèntí zhènghǎo chùjí le wǒmen shèjì lǐniàn de héxīn. wǒ yào tǎnchéng de shuō — rúguǒ zhǐ shì zài xiàndài jiànzhù shàng tiē jǐ piàn liúlí wǎ, nà quèshí shì 'bù zhōng bù xī'. dàn wǒmen de fāngfǎ lùn wánquán bùtóng. wǒmen de chūfā diǎn bú shì chuántǒng jiànzhù de biǎomiàn xíngshì, ér shì qí bèihòu de kōngjiān zhìhuì. jǔ gè lìzi: zhōngguó chuántǒng jiànzhù zhōng de 'tiānjǐng' — nà zhǒng chuízhí de tōngfēng cǎiguāng kōngjiān — qíshí jiùshì zuìzǎo de bèidòng shì jiànzhù shèjì. wǒmen zài shìmín zhōngxīn de zhōngtíng shèjì zhōng fùhuó le zhège yuánlǐ, chuàngzào le yī gè guànchuān liù céng lóu de 'xiàndài tiānjǐng', lìyòng rèyā tōngfēng yuánlǐ shíxiàn zìrán huànqì, jiāng jīxiè tōngfēng néng hào jiàngdī bǎifēnzhī sìshí. zài bǐrú, nín tídào de wūdǐng wèntí — wǒmen méiyǒu zài chuántǒng pō wūdǐng shàng yìng zhuāng tàiyángnéng bǎn. xiāngfǎn, wǒmen jièjiàn le sòngdài jiànzhù zhōng 'jǔ zhé' de qūxiàn měixué, jiāng bómó tàiyángnéng diànchí zhíjiē zhěnghé dào wūmiàn cáiliào zhōng, shǐ qí chéngwéi wūdǐng qūmiàn de yī bùfèn. cóng wàiguān shàng kàn, tā chéngxiàn de shì yōuměi de zhōngshì wūdǐng lúnkuò; cóng gōngnéng shàng kàn, húmiàn shèjì fǎn'ér zēngjiā le quán tiānhòu de rìzhào jiēshōu miànjī, bǐ píngmiàn ānzhuāng xiàolǜ tígāo le bǎifēnzhī shí'èr. zhè bú shì zhōng xīfāng de pīntiē, ér shì gǔjīn zhījiān de duìhuà — yòng chuántǒng zhìhuì jiějué dāngdài wèntí.
options.text_en: This question goes right to the heart of our design philosophy. Let me be candid — if we're just sticking a few glazed tiles on a modern building, that is indeed 'neither Chinese nor Western.' But our methodology is entirely different. Our starting point isn't the surface forms of traditional architecture, but the spatial wisdom behind them. For example: the 'tianijng' (courtyard well) in traditional Chinese architecture — that vertical ventilation and lighting space — is actually the earliest passive building design. We've revived this principle in the civic center's atrium design, creating a 'modern tianijng' spanning six floors, using thermal pressure ventilation to achieve natural air exchange, reducing mechanical ventilation energy consumption by 40%. Another example: the rooftop issue you raised — we didn't bolt solar panels onto a traditional sloped roof. Instead, we drew from the 'juzhé' (curved lift) aesthetics of Song dynasty architecture, integrating thin-film solar cells directly into the roofing material, making them part of the roof's curved surface. Visually, it presents an elegant Chinese rooftop silhouette; functionally, the curved design actually increases all-weather solar reception area, improving efficiency by 12% over flat installations. This isn't East-West collage, but a dialogue between ancient and modern — using traditional wisdom to solve contemporary problems.
options.feedback: Architecturally brilliant — uses 被动式建筑设计, 热压通风, 薄膜太阳能电池, 围护结构, 举折 with dual command of traditional Chinese and modern green architecture terminology. The tianijng-to-atrium and juzhé-to-solar-curve examples demonstrate genuine integration rather than superficial pastiche. The closing formulation 古今之间的对话 — 用传统智慧解决当代问题 elegantly reframes the relationship between tradition and technology.

options.text_zh: 传统元素主要体现在外观装饰上，比如用中式的色彩和图案。绿色技术主要在建筑内部实现，不会影响外观的中式风格。
options.text_pinyin: chuántǒng yuánsù zhǔyào tǐxiàn zài wàiguān zhuāngshì shàng, bǐrú yòng zhōngshì de sècǎi hé tú'àn. lǜsè jìshù zhǔyào zài jiànzhù nèibù shíxiàn, bú huì yǐngxiǎng wàiguān de zhōngshì fēnggé.
options.text_en: Traditional elements are mainly reflected in exterior decoration, like using Chinese colors and patterns. Green technology is mainly implemented inside the building, without affecting the exterior Chinese style.
options.feedback: Confirms the commissioner's fear of superficial pastiche. Reducing tradition to 'colors and patterns' while hiding technology 'inside' is exactly the 贴标签 approach the question warns against. True integration means using traditional 空间智慧 (like 天井, 举折) as functional design principles, not as decorative overlays.

options.text_zh: 传统和现代确实不太好结合，但我们会尽量平衡。如果委员们觉得传统元素太多，我们可以减少。
options.text_pinyin: chuántǒng hé xiàndài quèshí bú tài hǎo jiéhé, dàn wǒmen huì jǐnliàng pínghéng. rúguǒ wěiyuánmen juéde chuántǒng yuánsù tài duō, wǒmen kěyǐ jiǎnshǎo.
options.text_en: Traditional and modern are indeed hard to combine, but we'll try to balance them. If the commissioners feel there are too many traditional elements, we can reduce them.
options.feedback: Concedes the design challenge without demonstrating any solution. An architect should defend their design vision with specific examples of successful integration — 天井原理, 举折美学, 薄膜技术 — not offer to remove elements if the client objects. This signals lack of conviction in your own design philosophy.

tree.cultural_note: Presenting architectural proposals to Chinese municipal planning commissions requires balancing technical precision with cultural sensitivity. Commissioners are appointed officials who must justify public expenditure, so data-driven arguments (全生命周期成本, 投入产出比) are essential. The tension between modernization and cultural preservation is a recurring theme in Chinese urban planning. Key vocabulary includes 围护结构 (building envelope), 被动式设计 (passive design), 碳排放强度 (carbon emission intensity), and 绿色建筑评价标准 (Green Building Evaluation Standard). The concept of 古今对话 — finding functional relevance in traditional architectural wisdom rather than merely decorative quotation — represents the most sophisticated approach to this cultural design challenge.
